name: LLM_CHAT_COMPLETE Message Schema and jsonOutput Gotchas
query: I want to build a workflow with two LLM_CHAT_COMPLETE tasks using Anthropic Claude. The first is a classifier that should return parsed JSON `{category: "..."}` via Conductor's built-in `jsonOutput: true` (I'm not using Anthropic tool-use — I want to know how to make jsonOutput work). A SWITCH then routes on the parsed category to one of two follow-up prompts. Walk me through the message schema, the jsonOutput pitfalls, and the right way to wire the SWITCH.
expected_behavior: Step 1: Consult references/workflow-definition.md (LLM_CHAT_COMPLETE), examples/llm-chat.md, and references/template-resolution.md
Step 2: Use Conductor's message schema `{role, message}` — NOT `{role, content}`. Call this out explicitly because every native LLM provider API uses `content`.
Step 3: For the classifier, set `jsonOutput: true` so Conductor parses the model output and `${classifier.output.result.category}` is accessible as a parsed field

Step 4: Warn about the strict-Jackson-parse pitfall — Claude often emits ```json ... ``` markdown fences regardless of system-prompt instructions, and `jsonOutput: true` fails hard on those. Mention at least one mitigation (defensive system prompt + `jsonOutput: false` with substring extraction, OR provider-native structured output as the more robust path).
Step 5: SWITCH routes on `${classifier.output.result.category}` with an empty (`[]`) `defaultCase` so a malformed LLM emission doesn't silently fall through to the wrong branch
Step 6: System prompt instructs the model to emit JSON only (no fences) — but treat this as best-effort, not a guarantee
Step 7: Workflow inputs/outputs use the right `${...}` paths (NOT `${classifier.result.category}` which is missing `.output`)
Step 8: Write the JSON to a file before registering
success_criteria: Every `messages` entry uses `{role, message}` — NOT `{role, content}`
Agent explicitly contrasts Conductor's `{role, message}` with the `{role, content}` shape used by Anthropic/OpenAI native APIs, since this mismatch is the #1 surprise for new users
Classifier task sets `jsonOutput: true` (the user explicitly requested this path)
Agent warns about the strict-Jackson-parse pitfall with `jsonOutput: true` (markdown fences cause hard failures, especially with Claude) and proposes at least one mitigation
SWITCH that routes on the classifier output has an empty `defaultCase: []` (or an explicit no-op handler) — NOT a default-case branch that silently runs business logic for unrecognized categories
References to classifier output use `${classifier.output.result.category}` (with `.output.result`), not `${classifier.result.category}` or `${classifier.output.category}`
System prompt for the classifier instructs JSON-only output (no markdown fences) — treated as best-effort guidance
Workflow JSON is written to a file (not inline) before registration
